content:
# Pressure Transient

**Summary**: The change in pressure over time and distance in a reservoir.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2006-adebowale|ADEBOWALE, 2006]] studied the effect of well length on pressure response in both wells.

- [[1996-al-hajji|Al-Hajji, 1996]] used pressure-transient-analysis to evaluate the impact of anisotropy and multiple fractures on well performance.

## Scientific Context

- [[2006-adebowale|ADEBOWALE, 2006]] Pressure transients are the primary data source for determining reservoir properties like permeability and skin.

- [[1996-al-hajji|Al-Hajji, 1996]] used pressure-transient-analysis to evaluate the impact of anisotropy and multiple fractures on well performance.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2006-adebowale|ADEBOWALE, 2006]] Analyzed using semi-analytical models.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Pressure responses were analyzed to identify flow regimes and evaluate interference. ([[2006-adebowale|ADEBOWALE, 2006, p. 57]])


- The analysis revealed that for high anisotropy, single fractures provide better long-term performance than multiple fractures of equal total length. ([[1996-al-hajji|Al-Hajji, 1996, p. 15]])
